In other projects
Appearance
From Wiktionary
run is on the Basic English 850 List.
rule - run - sad
run is one of the 1000 most common headwords.
rule - run - safe

Pronunciation

[change]
  • (UK) (US) IPA (key): /rʌn/ SAMPA: /r\Vn/
  • Audio (US)(file)
  • Audio (UK)(file)
  • Audio (AU)(file)

Verb

[change]

Plain form
run

Third-person singular
runs

Past tense
ran

Past participle
run

Present participle
running

  1. If you run, you go quickly, with both feet off the ground at one time.
    • Synonym: hurry
    • Antonyms: stroll and walk
    I must run.
    I like to run in the afternoon.
  2. If a machine runs, it works.
    • Synonyms: function and work
    The car runs well.
  3. If you run from someone or something, you try to get away from them.
    • Synonyms: flee, escape and run for it
    • Antonyms: stand up to and stay
    He ran from the cops.
  4. (transitive) If you run a place, you manage it.
    He ran the hotel together with his beloved wife.

Related words

[change]
  • runner
  • run by
  • run down
  • run for it
  • run out on
  • run over
  • run through

Noun

[change]

Singular
run

Plural
runs
  1. A trip made by a runner; compare to walk.
    I’m going for a run.
  2. (baseball) A run in baseball is when a batter safely touches home plate.
  3. (cricket) A run in cricket is scored when the batsmen run between the two wickets.
  4. (theatre) An unbroken course of performances or showings.
    The musical had a long run on Broadway.
